title: FAOSTAT API Vocabulary
description: Controlled vocabulary defining key concepts, codes, and terminology used across FAOSTAT APIs

Domain.definition: A thematic subject area within FAOSTAT covering a specific statistical topic. Each domain has a unique code (e.g. QCL for Crops and Livestock Products) used in API calls.

Area.definition: A geographic unit (country, territory, or regional aggregate) for which data is reported. Areas have FAO codes, M49 codes, ISO2, and ISO3 identifiers.

Item.definition: An agricultural commodity, product, or category for which data is collected. Items use FAO codes or CPC (Central Product Classification) codes.

Element.definition: A statistical measure or variable type for which values are recorded. For example, area harvested (ha), production quantity (tonnes), or yield (hg/ha).

Flag.definition: A data quality or source indicator attached to each statistical value. Flags denote whether values are official, estimated, imputed, projected, or missing.

values.Ag: Aggregate, may include official, semi-official, estimated or calculated data
values.F: FAO data based on imputation methodology
values.Im: Country data made official by FAO
values.M: Missing value; data cannot exist; not applicable
values.Q: Official data reported on FAO Questionnaire
values.R: Estimated data using trading partners database
values.W: Includes official, estimated, calculated, and unofficial data

OutputType.definition: The format in which API results are returned.

values.objects: JSON array of objects with named fields (default)
values.arrays: JSON array of arrays (header row + data rows)

AreaCodeSystem.definition: The coding system used to identify areas in API queries.

values.M49: UN Statistics Division M49 numeric codes

ItemCodeSystem.definition: The coding system used to identify items (commodities) in API queries.

Language.definition: Response language for labels and descriptions returned by the API.

DomainGroups.definition: Top-level thematic groupings that organize FAOSTAT domains by subject area.
